headline: U.S.-Iran Pause, Hormuz Reopens
detail: The U.S. and Iran have agreed to halt attacks and Hormuz shipping has resumed. This removes the immediate oil-spike and supply-shock fear that was pressuring risk sentiment this morning, giving tech and chip stocks a cleaner tape to trade on today.

headline: Samsung and SK Hynix AI Semiconductor Mega-Investments
detail: South Korea confirmed both chipmakers are pouring capital into AI and semiconductor mega-projects. This is directly relevant to AVGO and SMH — it validates the long-term AI chip demand story, but near-term it also signals intensifying competition in the supply chain.

headline: Baidu AI Chip Arm Eyes $50B IPO
detail: Kunlunxin targeting a massive Hong Kong listing signals China is aggressively building out its own AI chip ecosystem. For your chip-heavy book — AVGO, SMH — this is a medium-term competitive headwind worth tracking even if it doesn't move the needle today.

headline: Severe Weather Threatening AI Infrastructure
detail: Reports highlight that extreme weather is increasingly disrupting AI data center buildout and operations. This is an emerging risk for the AI capex cycle that underpins the bull case for names like AVGO and CRWD — worth watching as a slow-building headwind.
